title: A Simple Content Repurposing Workflow for Turning One Idea Into a Week of Posts

introduction: Creating consistent, authoritative content can feel overwhelming, especially when juggling multiple platforms and formats. Yet, a single well-chosen idea can fuel a whole week’s worth of posts — if you have the right system in place. This article presents a straightforward content repurposing workflow that helps you turn one core idea into LinkedIn posts, emails, short-form videos, carousel posts, and a blog article — all while keeping your message clear and impactful. It's about working smarter, not harder, so you maintain momentum and grow your authority without burnout.

content:
Creating content consistently is crucial for establishing authority, yet many consultants, creators, coaches, and small business owners struggle to produce fresh material daily. Instead of scrambling for new ideas, a content repurposing system lets you maximise the value of one well-crafted idea by adapting it across various formats and platforms.

## Step 1: Identify Your Core Idea
Start with a single, well-defined idea relevant to your audience. For example, a coach might choose "Overcoming procrastination in small business owners." This focus ensures your message is clear and targeted.

### How to Choose a Core Idea
- Reflect on common client questions or challenges.
- Use feedback from recent calls or social media comments.
- Analyse trending topics in your niche.

## Step 2: Outline Your Content Assets
Decide which content formats will best reach your audience throughout the week. A practical mix could include:

- **LinkedIn posts** (short, conversational updates)
- **Email newsletter** (deeper insights or actionable tips)
- **Short-form video** (quick, engaging clips for social platforms)
- **Carousel post** (visual, step-by-step content for LinkedIn or Instagram)
- **Blog article** (comprehensive exploration of the topic)

## Step 3: Create Your LinkedIn Posts
Start by breaking your core idea into 3-4 micro-topics or tips. For instance, "3 quick steps to combat procrastination." Write short posts that tease the main message, encouraging engagement and setting up the deeper content.

## Step 4: Draft Your Email Newsletter
Expand one or two of the LinkedIn tips into a concise email. Include actionable advice, a personal anecdote, or a client example to add authenticity. End with a clear call to action, such as inviting readers to reply or download a free resource.

## Step 5: Produce Short-Form Video Content
Repurpose the LinkedIn tips into short videos (30-60 seconds) that visually demonstrate your points. Use simple tools like a smartphone and basic editing apps. Videos increase reach and engagement, especially on platforms like Instagram Reels or TikTok.

## Step 6: Design a Carousel Post
Turn your tips into a carousel — a series of slides that tell a story or outline steps. Keep text concise and visuals clear. This format works well on LinkedIn and Instagram, providing bite-sized learning.

## Step 7: Write the Blog Article
Finally, develop a detailed blog post that covers the entire core idea comprehensively. Use headings, examples, and actionable points. This asset serves as a cornerstone piece that can be linked back to from all other formats.

## Real-World Example
Imagine you’re a small business consultant focusing on time management. Your core idea: "How to structure your day for maximum productivity."

- LinkedIn Posts: Share 3 short tips like prioritising tasks, time-blocking, and avoiding multitasking.
- Email: Dive deeper into time-blocking with a step-by-step guide.
- Video: Record a quick demo of your own daily schedule.
- Carousel: Visualise the time-blocking method with easy-to-follow slides.
- Blog: Write a comprehensive article expanding on each tip with client stories.

This approach keeps your message consistent, builds momentum, and fills your content calendar with minimal extra effort.

## Key Takeaways
- One core idea can fuel a diverse range of content formats.
- Planning content with repurposing in mind saves time and reduces overwhelm.
- Maintaining message consistency across formats builds stronger authority.
- Practical tools and workflows, like GetContentOS, help organise and automate your system.
